ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Denii a baal ŋɔ, “Gil n kaali, bɔr chaanɔɔrɛ.” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Màá gil i kaali, di haŋ kissee lɛi.”
translated: Then he said, “Take your bows and quivers and go out into the fields to hunt.” And Jacob said, “You are my mother’s favorite, stay here."

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: U balaa ge faa kaa ki kɔlɔŋ bɛl a waluu biini di na niinanii.
translated: The hunter went to the farm to catch a chicken for his mother to cook.

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.
text: Wiizi ɔɔ kpumbuu wibaŋa ni, waari ge kpuŋ hamuŋa, maa ɔɔ wer n chɔ dɔɔ.
translated: The dog is very fierce, when it sees a stranger, it will surely bite

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Abraham faa dau a bɔr sɔŋ, ‘Kɔraawiizii Tɛɛri.’ A sɔŋ naa jaa birgi sɛɛliirɛ filaŋkeŋ, ba ɔɔ ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi bizeŋ nyuurɛ u ra tɛɛ.”
translated: Abraham went to a certain place, ‘Jehovah Jireh.’ And the place was later called by the name, because they said, “Jehovah will see on our behalf.”

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Denii Jekɔb kpa bii bɛl di baŋ,
translated: Today Jacob went to the farm
